market_sentiment.summary: 資料更新中，請稍後再試

articles.title: Everyone wants a piece of Tesla’s battery business
articles.description: Electricity demand from AI data centers is pushing everyone — including automakers like GM and Ford — into the energy storage business.

articles.title: Fresh off bond sale, Amazon borrows $17.5B from banks as AI spending continues
articles.description: Companies are burning through exorbitant sums of money to keep pace in the AI arms race. Debt is climbing.

articles.title: North Koreans behind nearly half of US tech industry hacks, says CrowdStrike
articles.description: North Korean hackers posing as remote IT workers and recruiters remain a major threat to U.S., European, and Asian companies, accounting for about half of all attacks over the past 12 months.

articles.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
articles.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

articles.title: UK crypto advocates launch campaign against banks blocking exchange transfers
articles.description: Stand With Crypto UK says banks are restricting access to regulated exchanges even as policymakers seek to position the country as a hub for digital asset innovation.

articles.title: Pending Bank of Japan rate decision may impact Bitcoin price: Should traders prepare?
articles.description: Bitcoin’s average price response to Bank of Japan rate hikes was a 22.5% sell-off. Will BOJ’s upcoming policy decision trigger another BTC price crash?

articles.title: Privacy returns to focus as Ethereum developers explore new token standards
articles.description: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we're looking at where privacy is headed in the Ethereum ecosystem.

articles.title: BlackRock and Fidelity are quietly turning bitcoin ETFs into a two-firm market
articles.description: BlackRock's IBIT and Fidelity's FBTC are attracting the vast majority of new bitcoin ETF money, leaving smaller funds increasingly sidelined as institutional investors consolidate around the industry's largest players.

articles.title: Coinbase-backed Stand With Crypto calls on members to campaign against banks blocking digital asset transactions
articles.description: Stand With Crypto UK is telling its members to file formal complaints with high-street banks over sector-wide transfer bans.

articles.title: Anchorage backs Treasury’s GENIUS AML rules, seeks secondary-market sanctions clarity
articles.description: A public comment letter argues that regulated stablecoin issuers need clearer compliance standards to avoid sanctions risks tied to secondary-market activity.

articles.title: Netflix expands revamped mobile app across Asia and doubles down on kids’ gaming
articles.description: The media giant is pushing to expand its mobile and gaming business.

articles.title: ‘AI-pilled’ firms spend $7,500 per employee each month on AI
articles.description: The most AI-obsessed firms are spending roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI, per Ramp AI Index. That's not more than an engineer's salary — yet.

articles.title: The Indian government got cold feet on Starlink just before SpaceX’s IPO
articles.description: Problems with Starlink's India expansion could challenge SpaceX's IPO growth story.

articles.title: Crypto Long & Short: Who answers the 3am call when DeFi breaks?
articles.description: In this week's Crypto Long & Short, Ben Nadareski writes that to win over big investors, DeFi builders must act like accountable money managers, not just software developers. Then, Stephen Stonberg says that bitcoin holders can survive crashes and protect their assets by earning income through reins

articles.title: Netomi CEO says $5 trillion AI customer experience market could boost stablecoin demand
articles.description: Puneet Mehta, a former high-level Wall Street engineer and data scientist, said the rapid expansion of AI enterprise software will increase demand for stablecoins and blockchain.

articles.title: How memory tools can make AI models worse
articles.description: New research suggests that AI memory systems can degrade model performance and encourage sycophantic tendencies.

articles.title: The quantum clock is ticking: it's Bitcoin's problem, not Ethereum's
articles.description: A recent research note published by Citi analysts reached a conclusion about quantum risk that should give every institutional bitcoin holder pause, Tabar explains.

articles.title: Mastercard prepares for a future where AI agents make payments
articles.description: The company is working with Coinbase, Stripe and other companies to build trusted payment systems for AI-driven commerce.

articles.title: Bitcoin rises despite US inflation hitting 3-year high: Where will BTC price go?
articles.description: BTC's rebound shows signs of weakening under a string of technical resistance levels, raising the odds of a dip below $60,000 in June.

articles.title: Zest launches a restaurant discovery app powered by where people actually eat
articles.description: Backed by Alexis Ohanian’s 776 and Kindred Ventures, Zest uses transaction data and AI to generate restaurant recommendations based on users’ real dining habits and the places they frequent.
